title: Network statistics of the whole-brain connectome of Drosophila
methods: neuropil connection (NPC) degree-corrected stochastic block model
data_sources: C. elegans hermaphrodite and male connectomes

data_scope.organism: adult female Drosophila melanogaster (7-day-old, w1118 x Canton-S G1)

core_conclusion: The first complete adult-brain connectome of Drosophila melanogaster (127,978 neurons, 2,613,129 connections) reveals a highly interconnected small-world network (S=141) with a large rich-club organization (~30% of neurons). Recurrent and reciprocal motifs are systematically over-represented; excitatory-inhibitory (ach-GABA) pairs dominate reciprocal connections. Rich-club neurons sit close to sensory inputs and bridge hemispheric bottlenecks, suggesting they underpin efficient global integration.
why_it_matters: First synaptic-resolution whole-brain network analysis of any adult animal; reveals non-random circuit architecture with small-worldness far exceeding C. elegans; characterises rich-club at unprecedented scale (~30% vs. 4% in C. elegans); links topology to neurotransmitter chemistry; ships open queryable platform (Codex).
why_nature: Landmark first complete adult-brain connectome; cross-disciplinary breadth spanning graph theory, neuroanatomy, and neurotransmitter biology; combines fundamental discovery with open infrastructure; establishes a baseline for comparative neuroscience across species and individuals.
added_value_to_my_research: Motif-frequency priors as structural constraints in physics-informed GNN models over spatiotemporal networks
GNN-accelerated layout methods applicable to large urban mobility and community-detection graphs
Criticality framework for testing whether urban transport networks operate near connectivity phase transitions
Flow-based parcellation replacing anatomical/administrative boundaries with data-driven catchment areas for R2SFCA
Neuropil motif-fingerprint audit protocol adaptable to multi-scale urban network topology characterisation

related.title: Connectome-constrained networks predict neural activity across the fly visual system
related.relation: Companion paper using the same FlyWire connectome to constrain neural network models — a direct extension of motif-based structural priors

related.relation: GNN-based layout acceleration directly applicable to visualising the 127,978-node connectome interactively

related.title: Unsupervised pretraining in biological neural networks

related.relation: Foundation models for neural activity could leverage the connectome's motif fingerprints as structural priors for cross-stimulus generalisation

figures.caption: Whole-brain network properties: degree distributions, connected-component structure (93.3% in one SCC), robustness to node removal, and rich-club coefficient (cut-off at total degree 37)
Characterising reciprocal connections: edge-weight distributions, neurotransmitter breakdown, ach-GABA/ach-glut/ach-ach pair strengths, and exemplar reciprocal neuron pairs
Three-node motif distributions across the whole brain: absolute counts, frequency relative to ER null model, edge strengths, neurotransmitter compositions of feedforward loops vs. 3-unicycles
Large-scale neuron connectivity: broadcaster/integrator/balanced classification of rich-club neurons; neurotransmitter and cell-class distributions; percentile-rank proximity to sensory modalities
Neuropil-specific differences: exploded brain view, reciprocity per neuropil, neurotransmitter divergence, neuropil-specific highly reciprocal neurons (NSRNs), and interneuropil reciprocal-pair map
Differences in three-node motif prevalence across all 78 neuropil subnetworks relative to CFG null models; average edge strengths per motif type per neuropil
